title: SACRAMENTO, CA —

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The legislation AB 591 will mandate that all new and retrofitted EV chargers in California include universal connectors and be publicly accessible to all types of EVs.",
    "The implementation of universal connectors for EV chargers is essential for increasing driver confidence in California’s charging network.",
    "Ensuring accessibility of EV chargers is proposed to help reduce carbon emissions and meet California’s climate goals.",
    "Communities of color and low-income communities have historically been last to benefit from California’s clean transportation transition.",
    "AB 591 aims to make electric mobility more affordable and ensure that EV infrastructure is accessible to everyone in California.",
    "The federal action taken by the Biden-Harris administration outlines plans for creating convenient and reliable electric vehicle charging networks.",
    "The legislation will help facilitate the transition to zero-emission vehicles in California, with a goal that all new passenger vehicles sold by 2035 will be zero-emission.",
    "Charger connectors are currently fragmented across different vehicle and charger models, leading to inconvenience for EV drivers.",
    "The lack of compatible connectors among charging stations contributes to range anxiety, a significant barrier to EV purchases.",
    "The transportation sector is responsible for more than half of California’s carbon pollution and 80 percent of smog-forming pollution."
  ]
}
```
